Plaintiff Yingnan Li'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290(B), 2031.300(B), And 2033.280(B)
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDECONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW MAILHOT, Esq. of The Ottinger Firm, P.C. (Tel: 415-325-2088) for plaintiff;
Atty. SUSANNAH K. HOWARD, Esq. of O'Melveny & Myers LLP (Tel: 415-984-8700) for defendants.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Yingnan Li's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290(B), 2031.300(B), and 2033.280(B).

Today's hearing is a resetting of the 9/8/20 hearing. Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Counsel and the judge pro tem all appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Plaintiff's counsel is to provide the judge pro tem with an approved form of Order by the close of business on September 16, 2020 (via email sent to rennepar85@gmail.com).

The Court signs the parties' Stipulation and Order Protecting Confidential Documents and Information.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290(B), 2031.300(B), And 2033.280(B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDECONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW MAILHOT, Esq. of The Ottinger Firm, P.C. (Tel: 415-325-2088) for Plaintiff Yingnan Li;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF YINGNAN LI'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290(B), 2031.300(B), and 2033.280(B).

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Both counsel and the judge pro tem appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. Matter discussed by the Court and plaintiff's counsel.

The Court takes the matter under submission. The Court instructs plaintiff's counsel to email to rennepar85@gmail.com by the end of the week (with a copy to defendant) a proposed order which sets forth with specificity what plaintiff wishes the court to order in regard to responses to the interrogatotories, request for production of documents, and requests for admission.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Set For Trial On Dec-07-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to compel the complaint's first and third through seventh causes of action is ordered off calendar as moot. The Court grants plaintiff's request to dismiss those causes of action.

Counsel for Uber is required to prepare a proposed order, approved as to form, which repeats the substantive portion of the Court's ruling verbatim.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
